tasks.title: Create schema.ts — core primitives (I18nLabel, PermissionExpression, PermissionGate, OnMissing)
tasks.description:
Create apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts with the four core primitive Zod schemas.

What to create:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts (new file)

Schemas to implement:
- I18nLabelSchema: z.object({ es, en, pt }) all z.string().min(1)
- PermissionExpressionSchema: z.string().regex for exact/wildcard/universal forms
- PermissionGateSchema: z.array(PermissionExpressionSchema).min(1)
- OnMissingSchema: z.enum(['disable', 'hide'])

Export:
- All schemas as named exports
- Inferred TS types: I18nLabel, PermissionExpression, PermissionGate, OnMissing

Acceptance criteria:
- All four schemas parseable with valid inputs
- Invalid inputs produce descriptive errors
- Types inferred and exported

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/schema.primitives.test.ts
- Test valid/invalid I18nLabel (missing es, missing en, empty string)
- Test PermissionExpression regex (exact, FOO_*, *, invalid 'foo', 'FOO*')
- Test PermissionGate empty array rejection
- Test OnMissing unknown value rejection
- AAA pattern, Vitest

tasks.title: Create schema.ts — Section schema + SectionSchema type
tasks.description:
Add SectionSchema to apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts.

Files affected:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts (extend)

Schema to implement (per doc 02 §4):
- SectionSchema: z.object({ id, label: I18nLabelSchema, icon, route: startsWith('/'), defaultRoute optional, sidebar: z.string().nullable() })
- Export Section type

Acceptance criteria:
- route must start with '/'
- sidebar can be null (no sidebar) or a string reference ID
- defaultRoute falls back to route if omitted

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/schema.section.test.ts
- Test valid section with sidebar = null
- Test valid section with sidebar = 'inicioSidebar'
- Test rejection of route without leading slash
- Test omitted defaultRoute defaults correctly

tasks.title: Create schema.ts — Sidebar items discriminated union (LinkItem, GroupItem, SeparatorItem, SidebarSchema)
tasks.description:
Add sidebar item schemas to apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts.

Files affected:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts (extend)

Schemas to implement (per doc 02 §5):
- ItemBaseFields const (id, label, icon optional, permissions optional, onMissing default 'disable', badge optional)
- LinkItemSchema: type literal 'link' + base fields + route startsWith('/') + exact boolean default false
- SeparatorItemSchema: type literal 'separator' + id only (no permissions)
- GroupChildItemSchema: discriminatedUnion([LinkItemSchema, SeparatorItemSchema]) — NO nested groups
- GroupItemSchema: type literal 'group' + base fields + defaultOpen boolean + items array of GroupChildItem min 1
- SidebarItemSchema: discriminatedUnion([LinkItemSchema, GroupItemSchema, SeparatorItemSchema])
- SidebarSchema: z.object({ items: z.array(SidebarItemSchema).min(1) })

Export all types: LinkItem, GroupItem, SeparatorItem, GroupChildItem, SidebarItem, Sidebar

CRITICAL: Groups CANNOT contain other groups (UX anti-pattern, doc 02 §5 locked decision)

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/schema.sidebar.test.ts
- Test link item with/without permissions
- Test group item with mixed children (link + separator)
- Test rejection of nested group inside group
- Test separator requires id, not label
- Test sidebar with min 1 item enforcement

- TabsConfigSchema: z.object({ entity: z.string().min(1), tabs: z.array(TabSchema).min(1).max(9) })

CRITICAL: max 9 tabs per entity (IA doc §5 design rule, doc 02 §6 locked)

tasks.title: Create schema.ts — WidgetSchema, DashboardSchema, TopbarConfigSchema, MobileConfigSchema
tasks.description:
Add widget, dashboard, topbar, and mobile schemas to apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts.

Files affected:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts (extend)

Schemas to implement (per doc 02 §7, §8, §9):
- WidgetScopeSchema: z.enum(['own', 'all', 'toggle'])
- WidgetTypeSchema: z.enum(['kpi','list','chart','feed','callout','shortcut','map','calendar'])
- WidgetSchema: { id, type, label, permissions optional, scope default 'all', config z.record(z.unknown()) optional }
- DashboardSchema: { widgets: z.array(WidgetSchema).min(1) }
- QuickCreateSchema: z.union([z.literal('all'), z.array(z.string().min(1)).min(1)])
- TopbarConfigSchema: { showSearch: boolean, showQuickCreate: QuickCreateSchema.nullable(), accountInMenu: boolean }
- MobileConfigSchema: { bottomNav: z.array(z.string()).min(2).max(5).nullable(), fab: z.string().nullable() }

Export: Widget, Dashboard, TopbarConfig, MobileConfig, WidgetScope, WidgetType types

Test requirements:
- Part of apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/schema.dashboard.test.ts
- Test MobileConfig bottomNav min(2) enforcement
- Test MobileConfig bottomNav max(5) enforcement
- Test bottomNav null = hamburger only (valid)
- Test TopbarConfig showQuickCreate as 'all', as array, as null

CRITICAL: Every link item route must point to a REAL existing admin route (spec §11.2)

tasks.title: Create permission-bundles.ts — expandPermissions() with wildcard support
tasks.description:
Create apps/admin/src/config/ia/permission-bundles.ts with the expandPermissions function.

Files affected:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/permission-bundles.ts (new file)

Implementation (per doc 02 §3.2):
- expandPermissions(expressions: PermissionExpression[]): PermissionEnum[]
- Handles '*' (expand ALL PermissionEnum values)
- Handles 'FOO_*' (expand all PermissionEnum starting with 'FOO_')
- Handles exact PermissionEnum value (validate + add)
- Throws Error for unknown permission: 'Unknown permission: ${expr}'
- Uses Set<PermissionEnum> for deduplication
- Returns [...result] as readonly-safe array

IMPORT: PermissionEnum from @repo/schemas

Export: expandPermissions function

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/permission-bundles.test.ts
- Test '*' expands to full PermissionEnum list (length > 0)
- Test 'ACCOMMODATION_*' expands to all ACCOMMODATION_ prefixed perms
- Test exact 'ACCESS_PANEL_ADMIN' is included once
- Test 'TYPO_*' prefix with no matches returns empty array
- Test unknown exact permission throws Error with message
- Test deduplication (same perm listed twice = only once in output)
- AC-4 coverage: verify '*' expansion works and non-empty result

tasks.title: Create index.ts — config composer (assembles all data files into raw AdminIAConfig object)
tasks.description:
Create apps/admin/src/config/ia/index.ts that assembles all data files without running validation (validate.ts does that).

Files to create:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/index.ts

Responsibilities:
- Import sections from ./sections
- Import sidebars from ./sidebars
- Import dashboards from ./dashboards
- Import tabs from ./tabs
- Import createActions from ./create-actions
- Import role configs from ./roles/host, ./roles/editor, ./roles/admin, ./roles/super-admin, ./roles/sponsor, ./roles/client-manager
- Assemble rawConfig object typed as AdminIAConfig
- Re-export AdminIAConfig type for consumers
- Import and trigger validatedConfig from ./validate (so importing index.ts triggers boot validation)
- Export: validatedConfig as the default-use config (named export 'config'), rawConfig for testing purposes

Per doc 02 §2: importing index.ts triggers validation. Invalid config = app does not start.

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/index.test.ts
- Test rawConfig has all 7 sections
- Test rawConfig has all 6 roles (4 enabled + 2 disabled)
- Test rawConfig has 4 dashboards
- Test rawConfig has 4 create actions
- Test config (validatedConfig) equals rawConfig when config is valid

tasks.title: Implement 7 cross-reference validations in AdminIAConfigSchema.superRefine() (was 9; §13.7+§13.9 dropped per spec §11.4)
tasks.description:
Add all 9 cross-reference validations to AdminIAConfigSchema.superRefine() in schema.ts.

Files affected:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts (extend AdminIAConfigSchema with .superRefine())

Validations to implement (per doc 02 §13, all 9):
1. §13.1 Sidebar refs: section.sidebar (when not null) must exist in sidebars
2. §13.2 Role mainMenu: each sectionId must exist in sections
3. §13.3 Role dashboard refs: dashboard must exist in dashboards
4. §13.4 Create action refs: topbar.showQuickCreate array items + mobile.fab must exist in createActions
5. §13.5 mobile.bottomNav refs: each sectionId must be in role's mainMenu
6. §13.6 labelOverrides paths: key must resolve to a known sidebar item or section (walk sidebar trees)
7. §13.7 Permission expansion sanity: expandPermissions() on each enabled role's defaultPermissions must be non-empty
8. §13.8 Unique IDs within sidebars: recursively check no duplicate IDs in each sidebar
9. §13.9 Universal wildcard '*' restricted to SUPER_ADMIN only

Each validation: ctx.addIssue with exact path array and descriptive message.

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/cross-reference-validations.test.ts
- One test per validation (min 9 tests)
- Each test constructs a minimal invalid config that triggers that specific validation
- Test asserts the error message contains the expected path and description
- AC-3: All 9 cross-reference validations tested

tasks.title: Create validate.ts — boot validation entry point with formatted error output
tasks.description:
Create apps/admin/src/config/ia/validate.ts — the boot-time validation entry.

Files to create:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/validate.ts

Implementation (per doc 02 §14):
- Import AdminIAConfigSchema from ./schema
- Import rawConfig from ./index (raw unvalidated config)
- IIFE that calls AdminIAConfigSchema.safeParse(rawConfig)
- On failure: format each issue as '  {path.join('.')}: {message}' joined by newline
- Throw new Error with format: '[admin-ia.config] Validation failed:\n{formatted}\n\nFix the config in apps/admin/src/config/ia/ and restart.'
- On success: export validatedConfig = result.data

CRITICAL: Runs at module load — importing validate.ts from index.ts means any consumer triggers validation at startup.

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/validate.test.ts
- Test with valid config: exports validatedConfig without throwing
- Test with bad sidebar ref: error message contains 'sections.catalogo.sidebar' and 'not found'
- Test with wildcard used by non-SUPER_ADMIN: error message contains expected path
- Test error message starts with '[admin-ia.config] Validation failed:'
- AC-2: Boot validation crashes app with clear multi-line error pointing to offending key

tasks.title: Create CI route-coverage script — assert every sidebar link points to a real route
tasks.description:
Create a script that validates every sidebar link item references a real TanStack Router route.

Files to create:
- apps/admin/scripts/validate-sidebar-routes.ts (new)
- apps/admin/package.json (add script 'validate:sidebar-routes')

Script logic:
1. Import rawConfig from src/config/ia/index.ts
2. Collect all REAL TanStack routes by listing apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/**/*.tsx files and parsing their route path from file name (e.g., accommodations/index.tsx -> /accommodations, $id.tsx -> /accommodations/$id)
3. For each sidebar in rawConfig.sidebars, for each link item (recursively in groups), extract item.route
4. Strip any query strings (e.g., /posts?status=draft -> /posts)
5. Assert the base path exists as a real route (or is a parent prefix of a real route)
6. On failure: print all bad links, exit with code 1
7. On success: print summary, exit 0

Add to package.json scripts: '"validate:sidebar-routes": "tsx apps/admin/scripts/validate-sidebar-routes.ts"'

Test requirements:
- No automated test file for the script itself, but running 'pnpm validate:sidebar-routes' from CI must exit 0 with the real config
- AC-22 support: this is the regression gate for reachable pages (spec §7 risk mitigation)

tasks.title: Create useCurrentSection and useCurrentSidebar hooks reading from IA config
tasks.description:
Create two custom React hooks that read from the IA config to determine the active section and sidebar.

Files to create:
- apps/admin/src/hooks/use-current-section.ts (new)
- apps/admin/src/hooks/use-current-sidebar.ts (new)

useCurrentSection():
- Uses useLocation() from TanStack Router
- Iterates config.sidebars to find which section's sidebar contains a link matching the current pathname
- Returns the matching Section object or null
- Memoize result per pathname
- CRITICAL: Uses sidebar membership for active-section detection (not route prefix), per spec §11.2

useCurrentSidebar():
- Calls useCurrentSection() to get active section
- Returns config.sidebars[section.sidebar] or null if no sidebar

Both hooks import from apps/admin/src/config/ia/index.ts (the validated config).

Export: { useCurrentSection }, { useCurrentSidebar } as named exports

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/hooks/__tests__/use-current-section.test.ts
- Mock useLocation to return various pathnames
- Test '/accommodations' resolves to 'catalogo' section
- Test '/posts' resolves to 'editorial' section
- Test '/billing/plans' resolves to 'comercial' section
- Test '/dashboard' resolves to 'inicio' section
- Test unknown path resolves to null
- AC-15 coverage: sidebar reads from useCurrentSection + useCurrentSidebar

tasks.title: Create useVisibleSidebarItems hook — applies onMissing filter with user permissions
tasks.description:
Create the hook that applies permission cherry-pick logic to sidebar items.

Files to create:
- apps/admin/src/hooks/use-visible-sidebar-items.ts (new)

useVisibleSidebarItems({ items, userPermissions, expandedPermissions }):
- Input: SidebarItem[] + user's expanded permissions array
- For each item: check if user has ANY of item.permissions (OR logic per PermissionGateSchema)
- If user lacks permissions AND onMissing='hide': exclude item from output
- If user lacks permissions AND onMissing='disable': include item with disabled:true marker
- For groups: if ALL children resolve to 'hide' -> hide the group; if some 'disable' -> render group with those items greyed
- Returns: ProcessedSidebarItem[] (SidebarItem extended with optional disabled:boolean)
- Memoize result

CRITICAL: OR logic for permissions (user passes if they have ≥1 of the listed permissions)

Export: useVisibleSidebarItems, ProcessedSidebarItem type

Test requirements:
- apps/admin/src/hooks/__tests__/use-visible-sidebar-items.test.ts
- Test item with onMissing:'hide' and no permission = excluded
- Test item with onMissing:'disable' and no permission = included with disabled:true
- Test item with required permission and user has it = included with disabled:false
- Test group where ALL children hide = group hidden
- Test group where SOME children disable = group present
- Test OR logic: user has 1 of 2 required permissions = item visible
- AC-16, AC-17 coverage

QuickCreate responsibilities:

tasks.title: Write boot validation tests — 7 invalid config crash scenarios (was 9 per spec §11.4)
tasks.description:
Write comprehensive tests for the boot validation system.

Files to create:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/boot-validation.test.ts

Test scenarios (per doc 02 §15 sample crash examples):
1. Bad sidebar ref typo: sections.catalogo.sidebar = 'catalogoSdiebar' → error contains 'sections.catalogo.sidebar' + 'not found'
2. Role mainMenu non-existent section: EDITOR.mainMenu = ['inicio', 'analyiss', ...] → error path 'roles.EDITOR.mainMenu.2'
3. Empty expanded permissions: HOST.defaultPermissions = ['ACCOMODDATION_*'] → error mentions 'Unknown permission'
4. bottomNav section not in mainMenu: HOST.mobile.bottomNav = ['comercial'] → path 'roles.HOST.mobile.bottomNav.0'
5. Duplicate IDs in sidebar: inicioSidebar with two items id='dashboard' → error mentions 'Duplicate item IDs'
6. Wildcard '*' for non-SUPER_ADMIN: ADMIN.defaultPermissions = ['*'] → error mentions 'SUPER_ADMIN role'
7. Enabled role missing dashboard: HOST without dashboard → superRefine catches 'dashboard ref required'
8. labelOverrides path to non-existent item → error about path not resolving
9. Dashboard ref doesn't exist: HOST.dashboard = 'missingDashboard' → error path 'roles.HOST.dashboard'

Test helper: createInvalidConfig(overrides) builder

AC-2, AC-3 coverage

tasks.title: Write expandPermissions unit tests — wildcards, exact, edge cases
tasks.description:
Write dedicated expanded unit tests for the expandPermissions function.

Files affected:
- apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/permission-bundles.test.ts (extend from T-011)

Additional test cases for full AC-4 coverage:
- '*' expands to every PermissionEnum value (assert count matches Object.values(PermissionEnum).length)
- 'ACCOMMODATION_*' expands to all ACCOMMODATION_ prefixed permissions only
- 'POST_*' expands to all POST_ prefixed permissions only
- Empty array input = empty output (no crash)
- Combo: ['ACCOMMODATION_*', 'POST_CREATE'] = ACCOMMODATION_* set ∪ {POST_CREATE} no duplicates
- Unknown exact permission 'NONEXISTENT_PERM' throws Error
- Prefix wildcard 'NONEXISTENT_*' expands to empty array (empty expansion)
- '*' cannot appear multiple times (idempotent via Set)
- AC-4 explicit test: verify '*' is ONLY valid via wildcard rule (not validated by expandPermissions itself, which is a runtime function — schema §13.9 validates the restriction)

tasks.title: Write permission cherry-pick visibility tests — disable/hide logic at L1/L2/L3

Test cases:
- L2 item onMissing:'disable' + user lacks permission → disabled:true in output
- L2 item onMissing:'hide' + user lacks permission → absent from output
- L2 item onMissing:'disable' + user has permission → enabled in output
- Group where ALL children are hidden → group itself absent
- Group where SOME children disabled → group present with those items disabled
- L1 (via MainMenu logic): section with 0 visible sidebar items → section not in mainMenu output
- Separator: always included if any sibling is visible
- Multiple permissions (OR logic): user has 1 of 2 required → item visible
- AC-16, AC-17 end-to-end coverage

Run full typecheck and lint after all migration tasks are complete. Fix any errors.

Steps:
1. Run 'pnpm --filter admin typecheck' — resolve all type errors
2. Run 'pnpm --filter admin lint' — resolve all Biome errors

Known Biome gotchas to watch for (per CLAUDE.md):
- useExhaustiveDependencies: pass whole objects as deps not individual properties
- noExplicitAny: use proper types, not 'any'
- useDefaultParameterLast: default params after required params
- noUnusedVariables: prefix unused vars with '_'

Acceptance:
- Zero TypeScript errors
- Zero Biome lint errors
- pnpm test passes (all tests green)

Test requirements:
- All existing tests still pass
- No .only() in test files
- Coverage report shows ≥90% on new files

tasks.title: Smoke test all existing admin routes — verify no regression in reachable pages (AC-22)
tasks.description:
Perform a full navigation smoke test across all major existing admin routes to verify no regression.

This is a MANUAL verification task (no automated test required — AC-22 is checked by T-020/T-036 script).

Steps:
1. Start the admin dev server
2. Log in as SUPER_ADMIN — verify 7 section items in header nav
3. Navigate to /accommodations — verify Catálogo sidebar loads
4. Navigate to /posts — verify Editorial sidebar loads
5. Navigate to /access/users — verify Comunidad sidebar loads
6. Navigate to /billing/plans — verify Comercial sidebar loads
7. Navigate to /settings — verify Plataforma sidebar loads
8. Navigate to /analytics — verify Análisis sidebar loads
9. Navigate to /dashboard — verify Inicio sidebar (2 items)
10. Navigate to /notifications — verify accessible (Mi inbox)
11. Log in as HOST — verify 5 section items, no Cmd+K, newAccommodation FAB
12. Log in as EDITOR — verify 4 section items, Cmd+K visible, 3 quick-create actions
13. Verify mobile BottomNav appears at <768px for HOST/EDITOR, absent for SUPER_ADMIN

Document any regressions as bugs to fix before marking this task done.

Acceptance: All routes navigable, correct section/sidebar per role, no JS errors in console.
